evidence: WordSketchApiServer.java is 988 lines and performs route registration, OPTIONS/method-check wiring, request parameter parsing, business-logic orchestration, and response-map assembly all in a single class. Ten handler methods are all private, making the class a sealed monolith.

SemanticFieldExplorer defines 7 public inner classes (ExplorationResult, DiscoveredNoun, CoreCollocate, ComparisonResult, AdjectiveProfile, Edge, ExploreOptions) that act as the data contract between the query and api layers. A class responsible for semantic field computation should not also own the serialization-facing data shapes.

BlackLabQueryExecutor exposes two public methods not on the QueryExecutor interface — findDependencyCollocations (line 139) and executeDependencyPattern (line 364). Callers that need dependency queries must reference the concrete type or receive no type-safe access. Main.java calls findDependencyCollocations via the executor cast.

The utils package contains four unrelated concerns: LongIntHashMap (data structure), LogDiceCalculator (statistics), CqlUtils (parser), PosGroup (enum). There is no cohesion principle unifying them beyond 'not fitting elsewhere'.
Package top-level names (api, config, indexer, query, tagging, utils, viz) map to recognizable domain capabilities. A new engineer can infer purpose from the package name.
README documents all 4 endpoints, build steps, and CLI commands that match runtime reality. The CLAUDE.md also reflects the actual package structure.

strengths: Package names (api, config, indexer, query, tagging, utils, viz) map directly to domain capabilities — a new engineer can orient quickly.
README and CLAUDE.md accurately describe the runtime structure, endpoint set, and CLI commands.
QueryExecutor interface cleanly localizes the corpus-query boundary.
high_level_elegance.issue_character: The dominant pattern is responsibility concentration: WordSketchApiServer absorbs all HTTP concerns for every endpoint, and SemanticFieldExplorer owns both computation and data-contract types, making both classes far harder to navigate and change than their logical roles require.
high_level_elegance.score_rationale: The top-level package decomposition is sound, and documentation is trustworthy — both would push the score higher. However, the 988-line god-class ApiServer, the absence of a model/DTO layer, and the two concrete-only methods on BlackLabQueryExecutor that escape the interface mean that change boundaries are not predictable at the class level. A codebase where each class's role is clear and change-impact is local would score 70+; this one sits in the mid-50s due to the scale of the ApiServer monolith and the ownership confusion around result types.

issues.summary: WordSketchApiServer is a 988-line god class mixing route registration, request parsing, business logic, and response building
evidence: 988 lines with 10 private handler methods: handleSketchRequest, handleFullSketch, handleRelationQuery, handleFullDependencySketch, handleDependencyRelationQuery, handleRelationQueryInternal, handleSemanticFieldExplore, handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti, handleSemanticField, handleSemanticFieldExamples, handleConcordanceExamples, handleVisualRadial, handleBcqlQueryPost.
Each handler method performs parameter extraction, validation, business-logic invocation, and response-map assembly. None of these concerns is separately testable without starting an HTTP server.
handleSemanticFieldExplore alone is ~120 lines (lines 437-557) building a response map field by field.
issues.suggestion: Extract request parsing and response building into dedicated handler classes or at minimum extract a response builder per logical feature area (SketchResponseBuilder, SemanticFieldResponseBuilder). Route registration belongs in a separate configuration method or a dedicated Router class. Each handler should receive already-parsed parameters and return a domain result, not a raw Map.

issues.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer owns 7 public inner result classes that serve as the api/query data contract, blurring class ownership
evidence: 7 public static inner classes defined inside SemanticFieldExplorer: ExplorationResult, DiscoveredNoun, CoreCollocate, ComparisonResult, AdjectiveProfile, Edge, ExploreOptions.
The class Javadoc explicitly acknowledges this: 'These should be extracted to a dedicated model/ package in a future refactor (see issue 28bc8918 / a430f585).'
WordSketchApiServer imports all 6 of these types to build API responses — the api layer's data shapes are governed by a class in the query package.
issues.suggestion: Create a pl.marcinmilkowski.word_sketch.model package and move all 7 inner classes there as top-level types. SemanticFieldExplorer returns model types; WordSketchApiServer imports from model, not query.

issues.summary: BlackLabQueryExecutor exposes findDependencyCollocations and executeDependencyPattern that are not on QueryExecutor, leaking concrete-type dependency to callers
evidence: findDependencyCollocations (line 139 of BlackLabQueryExecutor) is public but not declared on QueryExecutor.
executeDependencyPattern (line 364 of BlackLabQueryExecutor) is public but not on QueryExecutor.
Main.java calls executor.findDependencyCollocations(...) after casting the QueryExecutor reference to BlackLabQueryExecutor — the interface does not express the full contract.
issues.suggestion: Add findDependencyCollocations and executeDependencyPattern to the QueryExecutor interface (with proper Javadoc), or — if dependency queries are truly BlackLab-specific — introduce a sub-interface DependencyQueryExecutor that BlackLabQueryExecutor implements, so the type hierarchy reflects the actual capability boundary.

root_causes: No separation between HTTP plumbing and business logic led to the ApiServer monolith.
No model/DTO package caused result types to accumulate as inner classes inside compute classes.
Interface was designed conservatively (5 methods) and not expanded as new query capabilities were added.
likely_symptoms: The TODO comment in WordSketchApiServer about moving SEF imports is a symptom of the missing model layer.
